exercises.prompt: What is the main limitation of a standard, plain Generative Adversarial Network (GAN)?

options.text: They only generate black and white images
You cannot choose which specific category or class they generate
They require supercomputers to run even simple tasks

exercises.explain: GANs excel at making realistic items, but standard ones act like a blind lottery—you cannot pick what item they draw next!

cards.body: Imagine having a magic printer that spits out gorgeous photos of animals, but there is no keyboard. You press 'Print' and you get a random kitten, puppy, or bird. You can never ask it for a specific 'golden retriever'. That is the challenge of plain GANs!
cards.title: The Blind Printer

cards.body: A Plain GAN generates realistic samples from its training data, but it lacks the controls to let you request a specific output category (called a class). It simply turns random noise into random realistic outputs.
cards.title: What is Plain GAN?
The Label-less Vending Machine
cards.body: Think of a plain GAN like a vending machine with all its label buttons removed. You put in a coin (random noise), and a tasty snack drops out. It is always a good snack, but you cannot choose whether you get potato chips or chocolate!

If you train a plain GAN on handwritten digits (0 to 9), you can ask it to generate 'an image'. It might output a beautiful '7', then a '3', then a '0'. But you cannot type 'Give me a 5' and guarantee that a '5' will come out.
cards.title: The Digit Lottery

cards.body: Many people believe that because a GAN is smart, you can automatically tell it what to build. In reality, without extra helper structures, standard GANs are completely random and cannot take specific orders.
cards.title: Myth: Smart Means Control

left.text: Generates any random category from the training dataset.

right.text: Generates the exact item you ask for (like a brown cat).

exercises.explain: Standard GANs take random noise and turn it into random realistic images from the dataset. You have no steering wheel to select the class.
exercises.prompt: Why can't you ask a plain GAN to generate a specific digit like '9'?

options.text: They lack a way to guide the generator toward a specific target category.
They can only generate images that are blurry.
They require human labels during generation.

exercises.prompt: A plain GAN can generate realistic images of dogs, but you cannot force it to generate a Chihuahua on demand.
exercises.explain: True! Plain GANs map random noise to random data points. There is no input button or label parameter to specify the class you want.
Because plain GANs rely on random noise inputs without labels, their outputs are unpredictable, random samples.
exercises.sentence: A plain GAN takes noise and converts it into a _______ sample from the dataset.
exercises.prompt: Complete the description of a plain GAN's behavior:
Which case represents the output behavior of a plain GAN?
exercises.explain: Case B demonstrates a classic plain GAN limitation: it makes great digits, but they are completely random and cannot be selected on command.

options.text: Case A: You type 'cat' and the generator outputs a realistic cat image.
Case B: You run the generator and it outputs a random mixture of digits, with no way to select a '7'.
We must find a way to feed label information into both the generator and discriminator.
We must make the model much smaller.
We should stop using neural networks entirely.

exercises.prompt: If a plain GAN only outputs random images, how might we fix this so we can request specific things?
exercises.explain: Standard GANs lack control. Tomorrow, we will learn how we can feed extra label information into the network to guide the generation!

concept: Plain GANs generate random samples from the dataset; you cannot request a specific output class (like requesting a digit '7' vs a 'cat').
title: The Problem with Random Generation
